title: Per-lane ownership signal — extending the destructive-git-op guard to parallel /workflow lanes
summary: Grounds decision #2413: the #2367 destructive-git-op guard protects the serial two-session topology but fails open in the parallel /workflow topology, because parallel lanes are provisioned by position and never acquire a lease — and, empirically re-verified 2026-07-10, sibling lane agents share every ambient identity signal (a subagent inherits the parent's CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID verbatim; CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION is a constant flag). The survey (fencing tokens per Kleppmann, Kubernetes Lease holderIdentity, git worktree lock as the unchecked negative case, pid-file/flock failure modes, GitHub Actions concurrency groups, GNU Make's jobserver) converges on one principle: identity must be a minted string per logical holder, carried with each operation, and checked by the arbiter at the point of use — never inferred from an inherited ambient property. Two genuine forks survive the standing test. Fork 1 — where the lease-stamp lives: each lane agent's step-1 prep becomes `acquire --lane=N` with a per-lane LANE_SESSION slug (recommended — acquire already does the entire prep reset and fails loudly on a held lane), vs an orchestrator-side stamp at provision/coupling (duplicates coupling state, leaves crashed-before-dispatch leases). Fork 2 — what the guard compares when ownerSession cannot distinguish siblings: session-level ownership only (zero guard change, protects only against other top-level sessions), vs a per-lane assertion parsed from the command string of each destructive op, fail-closed (recommended — the fencing-token pattern; the guard already parses LANE_CLOBBER_OK=1/STALE_LANE_OK=1 from the command string, and the command string is empirically the only per-lane channel). The prep corrects the item's original framing: its acceptance (sibling op denied) is unreachable without a small guard-side comparison extension. Dissolved to supported-by-default: release lifecycle (TTL/stale-reclaim already shipped), serial + degraded fail-open unchanged, slug spelling <batchSlug>-<laneKey>. Round-2 (same day, post-land): the hooks payload’s experimental per-subagent agent_id channel was surfaced (via PR #402’s ev.session_id hint), docs-verified, skeptic-attacked as a candidate default — and refuted (the acquire-side O_EXCL owner stamp cannot see the payload-only field; the PreToolUse hook fires before acquire’s outcome exists, so a sidecar is poisoned by the live modulo-wrap collision; absence degrades silently to shared-session scope, the r2 fail-open pattern) — recorded as Fork 2 (c), a named probe-gated excluded branch; the default stays (b). Full grounding in reports/2026-07-10-per-lane-ownership-signal-parallel-workflow-lanes.md.
